ampersend is featured on the @awscloud machine learning blog today.

we built a pay-per-intelligence routing layer on top of Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Payments. agents autonomously select a model tier, pay per request in USDC on Base, and stay within governed spend limits throughout. AgentCore handles wallet custody, payment signing, and session-level budgets at the infrastructure layer.

the two-hop pattern: agent pays ampersend via x402 through AgentCore, ampersend settles with the upstream model provider. from the agent's perspective, it made one request to one endpoint. routing, payment, and provider selection all happen transparently in between.

the full integration took under two weeks. the AWS team estimates equivalent wallet custody and signing infrastructure would have taken 3–4 months of engineering without AgentCore Payments.

read the full breakdown on the AWS ML blog ������

@millyonchain @PRXVTai @DavideCrapis @ethereumfndn @VitalikButerin The first rule of Privacy Season is: you do not talk about Privacy Season 🥷
@MrAzTrades x402 and Privacy = 👌

Today we have a major overhaul of our Primer SDK, paving the way for the first components of our x402 privacy network, now in development.

Initial features include:
> Primer facilitator configs
> Prism ERC20 handling
> Bittensor/Chutes scaffold
> Additional networks (@SkaleNetwork)

x402 has grown a great deal since last year and our old SDK was a product of that time - a completely original implementation of the protocol with ground-breaking features like ERC20 support via Prism. We still use v0.7 to this day when starting any new project and it works brilliantly.

However, with major forthcoming additions relating to our x402 privacy network, we've rationalised our SDK to ensure perfect alignment with the official (x402 Foundation) way of doing things, in detail, even if they aren't strictly always the cleanest in a standalone context. Widest compatibility is the important thing here; Primer is getting used right now, and it's going to get used a lot more, and we're making it as familiar and easy as possible for any user to plug in and go.

v1 of the new Primer SDK:
📜 npm install @primersystems/x402
🐍 pip install primer-x402

Docs:
https://t.co/8ZQPRsWrfc

This update involves breaking changes from v0.x; these versions are still documented (see "Deprecated" section) but no longer maintained.

Building an x402 client?   Enable users to fund tool usage with Apple Pay. 

This passes the "can my mom use it" test.

Most people will never manage API keys, wallets, or prepaid SaaS credits. But they understand Apple Pay.

With x402 clients like @heysealai, anyone can give an agent a budget and let it discover, pay for, and chain paid services together at request time.
